content.theorem: The Eisenstein integers Z[ω], where ω = e^{2πi/3} = (-1 + i√3)/2, form a hexagonal lattice in the complex plane that is the densest 2D packing
content.proof: Every Eisenstein integer has the form a + bω where a,b ∈ Z. In the complex plane, these form equilateral triangles. The fundamental parallelogram has area √3/2. By Gauss's theorem, this is the densest lattice packing in 2D. The hexagonal lattice is its dual.
content.benchmark: 56/56 snap tests pass. Every point in the complex plane is within distance √3/3 ≈ 0.5774 of a lattice point. This is provably optimal.
content.caveat: Only works in 2D. Higher dimensions (E8, Leech) have different optimal lattices. The Eisenstein integers are special to dimension 2.
